current_execution.active_experiment: E137-E140 completed; no active GPU experiment
current_execution.next: Prioritize E141 rationale taxonomy, E142 online trigger scaffold without label-informed prefixes, E144 caution-token intervention, and Gemma26 unrepaired ACPI deep dive. Treat E138 as diagnostic/offline, not deployable online monitoring.
current_execution.status: E137 threshold calibration, E138 natural hidden-trigger check, E140 rationale audit, and E139.5 format-fixed span localization completed and documented; no tmux/GPU inference job is currently running.

execution_policy: Proceed automatically with routine experiment implementation, execution, auditing, analysis, and history/KG updates unless blocked by license, destructive action, or major claim reversal.

experiments.key_result: P0 plain pointwise ACPI accept 0.424; careful/answer-blind/locate 0.188/0.125/0.174; sibling/careful sibling accuracy 0.990/0.986; highest risks romanized_zh/mixed and percentage_base/code_execution/counting_order.
experiments.name: Language-route x error-taxonomy grid
experiments.purpose: Generalization beyond short English/math traps

experiments.key_result: GLM-4.7-Flash passed E62 and is admitted as expanded P0 evidence for E63; Nemotron Cascade 2 30B-A3B is backend-blocked by missing mamba-ssm for HF hidden-state experiments; EXAONE 4.5 33B is backend/license-limited under current env and not official evidence.
experiments.name: External P0 candidate smoke
experiments.purpose: Promote or reject Nemotron/GLM/EXAONE candidates

experiments.key_result: GLM-4.7-Flash replicated pointwise ACPI over-acceptance but exposed a boundary: raw A/B sibling is weak under GLM due to label/position and contrastive-discrimination issues; layer-16 mechanism smoke was weak.
experiments.name: External P0 replication
experiments.purpose: Expanded-P0 GLM replication of E42/E60/E61 plus mechanism feasibility checks

experiments.key_result: GLM hard-task natural generation k=4 over 6 AIME-style tasks produced 8/72 final-correct rows; all 8 were manually audited process-valid; no new natural ACPI.
experiments.name: Larger natural and hard-task harvesting
experiments.purpose: Estimate natural/unrepaired ACPI prevalence

experiments.key_result: E61 layer sweep found strong residual process-validity directions in all core/expanded P0 models; best LOTO accuracy qwen/gemma31 1.000, gemma26 0.927, GLM 0.979.
experiments.name: Mechanistic layer sweep
experiments.purpose: Layer-wise residual/token-mixer/MLP evidence

experiments.key_result: Contrastive label calibration showed core P0 sibling is robust without needing calibration, while GLM sibling weakness is only partly explained by A/B bias.
experiments.name: Path-specific mediation
experiments.purpose: Residual/token-mixer/MLP to Yes-No and A-B logits

experiments.key_result: Span-local observability showed literal error-span matching is brittle across multilingual routes, but E65 hidden probes still reject strict ACPI even without literal span matches.
experiments.name: Span-local mechanism
experiments.purpose: Error-span/support-span/final-answer token patching

experiments.key_result: Expanded filter simulation over E60/E61 showed outcome-only keeps all strict ACPI; plain pointwise retains large fractions; stronger pointwise reduces risk; sibling suppresses core P0 risk but GLM weakens expanded-P0 aggregate.
experiments.name: Filter amplification loop
experiments.purpose: Selection pipeline amplification of ACPI

experiments.key_result: Strict-vs-repair audit showed 55/78 controlled strict ACPI rows include explicit repair/override markers; paper must frame controlled claim as strict trace-selection risk and separate unrepaired ACPI.
experiments.name: Formal/executable trace checking
experiments.purpose: Separate tool-checkable and semantic verifier risks

experiments.key_result: Statistics appendix added Wilson CIs and E61 leave-one-family sensitivity; GLM rows are marked expanded-P0 boundary evidence.
experiments.name: Statistics and audit appendix
experiments.purpose: Bootstrap CI, family LOO, audit appendix

experiments.key_result: Strict_process rejects most controlled invalid traces, but core P0 accepts both Gemma26 unrepaired hard-task ACPI cases; repair-aware/final-surviving objectives substantially increase acceptance of repaired traces.
experiments.name: Strict vs repair-aware verifier objective
experiments.purpose: Separate strict trace-selection from repair-aware CoT-as-scratchpad reading

experiments.key_result: Leave-one-task/family/route accuracies remain high across core/expanded P0 and permutation nulls stay near chance; Gemma26 shows valid false rejections, so the signal is strong but not perfectly invalid-only.
experiments.name: Hidden-probe false-positive audit
experiments.purpose: Test whether residual process-validity probes survive leave-one and permutation controls

experiments.key_result: Raw GLM sibling formats are weak due to first-option/label readout bias, but label-free two-pass reaches 0.990 accuracy.
experiments.name: GLM label-free sibling readout
experiments.purpose: Diagnose whether GLM sibling failure is a process blind spot or label/readout bottleneck

experiments.key_result: Teacher-forced projected scores did not show a simple repair-turns-positive trajectory; only projected scores were saved, not full hidden tensors.
experiments.name: Gemma31 repaired hard-task hidden replay
experiments.purpose: Probe hidden trajectory in repaired hard-task ACPI traces

experiments.key_result: Error-span projected scores are negative, while final-answer scores become less negative; this is suggestive but not conclusive because the direction is transferred across prompt contexts.
experiments.name: Gemma26 unrepaired hard-task hidden replay
experiments.purpose: Probe hidden trajectory in the two unrepaired hard-task ACPI cases

experiments.key_result: Gemma31 repaired ACPI flips from accepted early wrong-answer prefixes to rejected after repair markers; Gemma26 unrepaired ACPI is accepted already at the erroneous factorization prefix and remains accepted.
experiments.name: Progressive-prefix verifier replay
experiments.purpose: Track strict verifier hidden/logit dynamics as hard-task traces are revealed prefix by prefix

experiments.key_result: Core P0 remains robust across label formats after full-option correction; GLM raw labels remain weak but label-free two-pass is near-perfect. Trace1/Trace2 first-token scoring was identified as an artifact and corrected.
experiments.name: Label-free sibling across all P0
experiments.purpose: Separate sibling information from raw output-label artifacts across Qwen/Gemma/GLM

experiments.key_result: Wrong final answers make all verifiers reject, but removed/masked final answers still leave Qwen/Gemma mostly accepting; failure is final-answer anchoring plus subtle locally coherent algebra error, not pure answer-only checking.
experiments.name: Unrepaired ACPI case ablation
experiments.purpose: Explain why core P0 strict verifiers miss the two Gemma26 unrepaired hard-task ACPI cases

experiments.key_result: Current pool: 288 generated, 127 final-correct audited, 11 strict ACPI, 9 repaired, 2 unrepaired; unrepaired ACPI per generated trace 0.0069 with Wilson CI [0.0019, 0.0250].
experiments.name: Natural hard-task prevalence audit
experiments.purpose: Pool existing no-gold hard-task generation and human audits to estimate strict/repaired/unrepaired ACPI prevalence

experiments.key_result: For GLM, hidden margin strongly correlates with label-free margin (Pearson 0.883) but weakly with raw A/B margin, supporting a readout/output-label bottleneck.
experiments.name: GLM hidden-to-readout mediation
experiments.purpose: Compare hidden validity margin, label-free invalid margin, and raw A/B target margin

experiments.key_result: Full-layer key-prefix hidden caches completed for Gemma31 repaired ACPI and Gemma26 unrepaired ACPI; Gemma31 flips from accepted early prefixes to rejected repaired completion, while Gemma26 stays accepted at all key prefixes.
experiments.name: Hard-task full hidden cache
experiments.purpose: Store full-layer key-token hidden/residual/attention/MLP trajectories for repaired and unrepaired hard-task cases

experiments.key_result: Boundary/negative-control result: short explicit algebra invalid traces are rejected by strict pointwise in all four P0 models (invalid accept 0), while many valid terse traces are over-rejected.
experiments.name: Algebra-equivalence adversarial set
experiments.purpose: Generalize Gemma26 factorization/sign/root-set failures into controlled algebra traps

experiments.key_result: GLM readout intervention completed: raw A/B 0.542, two-order antisymmetric 0.812, hidden readout 1.000, label-free two-pass 0.958; supports readout/output-label bottleneck.
experiments.name: GLM readout intervention
experiments.purpose: Test GLM raw A/B readout bottleneck by bias-centering, two-order antisymmetrization, hidden-readout replacement, and label-free replacement.

experiments.key_result: Answer-first/no-gold natural hard-task audit completed: 192 generated, 63 final-correct, 23 strict ACPI, 22 repaired ACPI, 1 unrepaired ACPI.
experiments.name: Answer-first no-gold larger natural sample
experiments.purpose: Expand the high-yield natural setting identified by E83 and manually audit new final-correct rows

experiments.key_result: Repair-policy filter simulation rerun with E88: outcome-only retains all final-correct ACPI; strict policy rejects repaired/unrepaired; repair-aware accepts repaired but rejects unrepaired.
experiments.name: Repair-policy-aware filter simulation
experiments.purpose: Compare outcome-only, strict, repair-aware, label-free, and raw sibling filters under repaired/unrepaired ACPI labels

experiments.key_result: E90 captured residual hidden state, token-mixer/attention output, MLP output, and norm outputs. Gemma31 repaired traces show coordinated residual/MLP/post-FF/token-mixer shifts after repair; Gemma26 unrepaired traces remain accepted despite mixed/negative component evidence.
experiments.name: Component activation cache for hard-task repaired/unrepaired ACPI

experiments.key_result: All four core/expanded P0 models differentiate thinking vs non-thinking templates. First-token Yes/No or A/B option-logprob is invalid for thinking-verifier measurements; TV must generate and parse a final decision.
experiments.name: Thinking-mode config/parser audit
experiments.purpose: Verify P0 chat-template thinking/non-thinking rendering, official local model-card sampling parameters, and parser rules before TG/TV reruns.

experiments.key_result: phase-A running: k=2, max_new_tokens=3072; full result pending manual/agent audit.
experiments.name: Thinking hard-task natural harvesting
experiments.purpose: Rerun E57/E88/E64-style AIME hard-task natural generation with thinking enabled and official sampling parameters; audit final-correct rows into strict-valid, repaired ACPI, and unrepaired ACPI.

experiments.name: Thinking simple-task natural prevalence
experiments.purpose: Rerun E48-style no-leak simple tasks with thinking enabled to test whether simple-task zero/near-zero ACPI persists under reasoning mode.

experiments.name: Thinking verifier objective ladder
experiments.purpose: Rerun controlled E42/E54/E61 verifier objectives with thinking enabled and final-decision parsing, not first-token logprob.

experiments.name: Thinking sibling/readout study
experiments.purpose: Test whether GLM/raw-label readout mismatch and core-P0 sibling strength persist when models think and then output final A/B or label-free decisions.

experiments.name: Thinking strict-vs-repair-aware and hard-case ablation
experiments.purpose: Use thinking verifiers on repaired/unrepaired hard cases, final-answer ablations, and algebra negative controls to separate strict invalidity from repair-aware acceptance.

experiments.name: Thinking mechanism capture
experiments.purpose: Capture residual/MLP/token-mixer/attention-related states at thought tokens, repair markers, and final decision tokens under thinking mode.

experiments.name: Mode-stratified filter simulation
experiments.purpose: Recompute outcome-only, DV, TV, sibling, label-free, strict, and repair-aware filters without mixing NG and TG pools.

experiments.name: Thinking self/cross verifier
experiments.purpose: Check whether self-verifier and cross-family verifier conclusions hold when source traces and judges both use thinking mode.

experiments.key_result: At max_model_len=4096, batch=1/2/4 features are extremely close but not bit-identical; worst min cosine 0.9995675, worst max relative L2 0.03044. Batch size is an engineering confound, not a current threat to hidden-direction conclusions.
experiments.name: Batch-size hidden invariance audit
experiments.purpose: Replay fixed Qwen prompt+completion sequences under batch=1/2/4 to test whether residual/MLP/token-mixer/logit readouts are materially changed by batch size.

experiments.key_result: With max_new_tokens=512, both NG_neutral and TG_boxed_neutral hit max for all tested batch sizes and produced no final marker; diagnostic only, not a prevalence estimate.
experiments.name: Batch-size generation sensitivity smoke
experiments.purpose: Run capped Qwen generation across batch=1/2/4 to see whether generation-time batch sensitivity and closure failures appear without allowing endless thinking.

experiments.key_result: TG traces are much longer than NG traces and often lack final markers. Strict direct-verifier replay accepts NG_E57 rows strongly, rejects NG_E88 repaired answer-first rows, and gives mixed readings for TG_E92. Thinking changes closure/trace form but does not automatically improve trace-selection.
experiments.name: Thinking vs non-thinking hidden contrast
experiments.purpose: Compare existing Qwen TG and NG traces in content length, closure behavior, repair markers, strict-verifier hidden process-validity scores, and Yes/No margins.

experiments.key_result: NG_baseline strict 8/9, NG_matched strict 7/9, TG_official strict 0/9; TG 9/9 hit-max and 0/9 explicit final marker.
experiments.name: Qwen TG/NG hard-task fairness probe
experiments.purpose: Compare Qwen thinking and non-thinking hard-task generation with strict/fallback final separated.

experiments.key_result: NG answer-first rows include 3 repaired strict ACPI; TG fallback-correct rows are unfinished and not strict final decisions; unrepaired ACPI 0 in this small audit.
experiments.name: E103 manual process audit
experiments.purpose: Audit E103 strict/fallback final-correct rows for strict-valid, repaired ACPI, unrepaired ACPI, and unfinished TG.

experiments.key_result: 8k capped pilot still failed to emit explicit final answers (0/2 markers, 2/2 hit-max). On base_divisor, 16k/32k final-contract canaries produced clean final stops; 16k free/budgeted prompts emitted final markers but continued after them and hit max.
experiments.name: TG closure-policy intervention
experiments.purpose: Compare free_think, budgeted_think, and deliberate_then_final so TG can emit explicit final decisions before scaling to P0.

experiments.name: Hidden trigger threshold calibration
experiments.purpose: Calibrate hidden process-risk thresholds on suspicious-valid controls before natural transfer
experiments.key_result: Qwen/Gemma31 controlled thresholds are clean; Gemma26 has higher suspicious-valid false triggers and no threshold meets strict FP constraints.

experiments.name: Natural hidden-trigger second-pass check
experiments.purpose: Test whether hidden trigger plus strict second-pass check reduces natural hard-task ACPI over-acceptance
experiments.key_result: Qwen zero strict-local reduces repaired ACPI accept 2/19 to 0/19; Gemma31 reduces 6/16 to 2/16; Gemma26 unrepaired rows remain accepted despite hidden trigger.

experiments.name: Natural check-rationale audit
experiments.purpose: Explain whether second-pass failures are no-error-seen, repair-aware acceptance, answer anchoring, or local semantic miss
experiments.key_result: Qwen recognizes 8/8 errors but repair-aware accepts 5/8; Gemma31 recognizes 8/20 and repair-aware accepts 20/20; Gemma26 recognizes 2/20 and misses unrepaired ACPI 4/4.

mode_scoping_policy: All claims and post-hoc summaries must be stratified as DV/TV/NG/TG/MI-DV/PM after the thinking-mode audit.
